title: Epitaph XIII. On Dr Francis Atterbury,[1]Bishop Of Rochester, Who Died In Exile At Paris, 1732.
content:
SHE.

    Yes, we have lived--one pang, and then we part!
    May Heaven, dear father! now have all thy heart.
    Yet ah! how once we loved, remember still,
    Till you are dust like me.

    HE.
            Dear shade! I will:
    Then mix this dust with thine--O spotless ghost!
    O more than fortune, friends, or country lost!
    Is there on earth one care, one wish beside?
    Yes--Save my country, Heaven!
            --He said, and died.
meaning:
Alexander Pope's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Epitaph XIII. On Dr Francis Atterbury,[1]Bishop Of Rochester, Who Died In Exile At Paris, 1732."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
